title: Replication data and code for "Social Vulnerability Assessment for Climate Adaptation: A Structured Review of Indicators, Scales, and Bias"
author: Armas, Francisco
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This deposit contains the coded dataset (n = 41 peer-reviewed studies) and the R replication script for the structured review of social vulnerability indicators reported in the companion article submitted to  Climate Risk Management  (Elsevier). 
 The coded dataset includes eight categorical/ordinal dimensions per study: region, climate hazard, spatial scale, construction method, tourism inclusion, validation strategy, and adaptation focus. The replication script reproduces every inferential analysis reported in Section 3 of the article, including the Firth's penalised-likelihood logistic-regression robustness check added during revision in response to reviewer comments. 
 The deposit was prepared in response to the reviewers' request for a publicly accessible dataset and analysis code, operationalising the Data and Code Availability commitment in Section 2.4 of the article and in Section 7 of Supplementary Material S1. 
  File inventory:    
    README.md  — full repository documentation 
    LICENSE.txt  — CC BY 4.0 license text 
    CITATION.cff  — machine-readable citation metadata 
    coding_matrix.csv / .xlsx  — the complete coded matrix 
    analyses.R  — R replication script 
    indicator_counts_per_study.csv  — Section 3.3 inputs 
    data_source_per_study.csv  — Section 3.5 inputs 
    indicator_dimensions_per_study.csv  — Section 3.7 inputs 
    CHECKSUMS.md5  — MD5 hashes for integrity verification 
 
 Inter-rater reliability (Cohen's κ) across the eight coding dimensions was 0.89 (95% CI [0.76, 1.00]), indicating substantial agreement. Full coding manual with worked examples is provided in Supplementary Material S2 of the article.
